topic: EXP-436: NOAA NP.xy cluster worked-example paragraph hoist (Part 3.1b) + WIN-006/007/022 post-hoc compressions
change_summary: EXP-436 appended a new "NOAA NP.xy cluster" worked-example paragraph at the end of Part 3.1b (#posthoc-npxy anchor) and replaced ~1000-char duplicated post-hoc paragraphs in WIN-006/007/022 detail_verdict_text with ~500-char 1-sentence references to it. WIN-036 explicitly NOT modified — separated on OTS-canary grounds.
our_argument_summary: Four WINs (006/007/022/036) draw post-hoc-confirming numbers from NOAA pole-position data (NP.xy series and the closely-related WMM); the cluster argument that this is publicly-indexed data, plus the hardcoded-target / no-monitor.py-entry monitoring patterns, applies to three of them as a single shared argument and is now told once in Part 3.1b instead of replicated four times in the WINs panels. WIN-036 stays standalone because its post-hoc failure runs through the OpenTimestamps canary (WMM2025 published the pole >14 months before PROS-006 registration), not through the hardcoded-monitor pattern.

appended at end of p3-31b ps-detail block
data/wins.json id=006/007/022 detail_verdict_text — post-hoc paragraph replaced with #posthoc-npxy reference
data/wins.json id=036 detail_verdict_text — UNCHANGED (explicit out-of-scope per EXP-436)
raw-text/02-wins.txt:19-22,68-72,107,124 — PROS-002 parent and WIN-006/007/022/036 confirmation rows
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Cluster paragraph synthesizes per-WIN dataset/monitoring claims already established in prior c5/c6 cycles for WIN-006 (live-fetch + hardcoded 0.0466 deg/yr target), WIN-007 (live-fetch + hardcoded 130°E threshold per c3, plus exponential decay formula per WIN-022 detail_evidence), WIN-022 (no dedicated monitor.py entry per c2), WIN-036 (OTS canary + WMM2025 pre-publication arithmetic per c5). The cluster framing is correct at the section-3.1b level of abstraction.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: No new code claims introduced. The cluster paragraph paraphrases prior-cycle findings without re-asserting numeric values that would need re-verification (it cites the dataset names, the WMM2025 release date, the >14-month delta, the >18° arithmetic — all already verified in prior cycles).
data_sources_used: NOAA NGDC NP.xy historical pole-position series (gufm1 pre-1900 + IGRF modern; IGRF-14 current)
World Magnetic Model WMM2025 (released Dec 17 2024, US/UK joint release)
Chulliat & Newitt 2010 EOS (DOI 10.1029/2010EO490001 — verified consistent with WIN-007 existing citation)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None new. The pre-existing per-WIN escape clauses (PROS-002 unbounded threshold, lack of order parameter) carry forward in the unchanged opening paragraphs of each WIN.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: One pre-existing internal tension surfaced: WIN-022 detail_evidence describes Domain #11 monitor.py exponential formula 55.0×exp(-0.08×(year-2015)) with 50% adaptive tolerance, while the new (and prior) compressed post-hoc reference says "no monitor.py entry exists for this value at all." Both can be defended (Domain #11 is shared with WIN-007 and does not specifically test phase-transition; no DEDICATED WIN-022 entry exists), but the surface contradiction is exploitable. Pre-existing — not introduced by EXP-436.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section-level review; not a per-WIN tag. Cluster paragraph correctly aggregates the per-WIN tags: WIN-006/007 live_fetch, WIN-022 none, WIN-036 hardcoded (per c5 reviews).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Section-level rhetorical paragraph; no per-section tag in this dimension.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Section-level prose; no per-section tag in this dimension.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Section-level prose; no per-section tag in this dimension.

holes_found.description: Opening sentence overstates source unity: "Four WINs … draw their confirming numbers from the same source: NOAA NGDC's North Magnetic Pole position record series (NP.xy)." WIN-036's confirming number (139.298°E) comes from WMM2025, which is a separate US/UK product (built on IGRF-14, related to NP.xy but not identical). The paragraph itself mid-sentence acknowledges WMM2025 for WIN-036, but the opening framing reads as if all four use NP.xy specifically. A dome defender could pick at this: "WMM2025 is not the NP.xy series; you're flattening two distinct NOAA products to make the cluster look tighter."
holes_found.recommendation: Soften opening to "draw their confirming numbers from the same family of NOAA pole-position products — the NP.xy historical series (NOAA NGDC) and the closely-related WMM (US/UK joint)" or similar. Optional polish; the rest of the paragraph already handles WIN-036's WMM2025 source correctly.

holes_found.description: WIN-007 compressed reference says "the dome's monitor.py hardcodes the post-1990 acceleration target on the same commit as the live-fetch line." Per WIN-007.c3 the actual hardcoded scalar in Domain #11 is the 130°E longitude threshold (trivially passing since the pole is at ~139°E), and per WIN-022 detail_evidence Domain #11 also hardcodes an exponential decay formula 55.0×exp(-0.08×(year-2015)) with 50% adaptive tolerance. "The post-1990 acceleration target" is a defensible umbrella phrase for either, but the original (pre-EXP-436) paragraph at least said "the target number" generically — calling it specifically an "acceleration target" suggests a numerical acceleration value is hardcoded, which is misleading. A defender could argue: "You imply we hardcode an acceleration number; we hardcode a longitude threshold that any model passes — those are not the same epistemic posture."
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten to either "hardcodes the longitude threshold (130°E, trivially passing) and the exponential-decay formula parameters on the same commit as the live-fetch line" — or fall back to the original generic "hardcodes the target number." The current "acceleration target" wording is the worst of both worlds: specific enough to mislead, vague enough to lose the trivial-pass strength.

holes_found.description: WIN-022 compressed reference says "no monitor.py entry exists for this value at all — the dome author has not included it in the monitoring suite." This preserves WIN-022.c2's monitoring=none tag. But WIN-022 detail_evidence (unchanged) opens with "Domain #11 in monitor.py uses a hardcoded exponential decay formula (55.0 × exp(-0.08 × (year-2015))) with 50% adaptive tolerance — the parameters are asserted, not fitted to any data." These two statements coexist in the WIN-022 panel and contradict on surface reading: one says monitor.py code exists for this WIN; the other says it does not. Pre-existing tension (not introduced by EXP-436) but now more visible because the compressed reference is shorter and bolder.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) reconcile WIN-022 detail_evidence with the compressed reference by clarifying that Domain #11 is shared with WIN-007 and does not test the phase-transition claim specifically (so "no dedicated monitor.py entry for WIN-022's phase-transition claim exists"), or (b) spin a separate ISS for the analyst to harmonize the two WIN-022 descriptions. Out of EXP-436 scope; flagging for the decider.

holes_found.description: Net char delta for Part 3.1b: prior region ~6605 chars → now 8726 chars (+2121, per EXP-436's stated +2121 hoist add). EXP's claim is "net +540 chars across the four touched fields" and that the compression target is TF-IDF dilution not char-count reduction. This is honest but worth flagging: total review size grew, not shrank. The TF-IDF argument is correct (one argument told once is rhetorically stronger than the same argument told three times in different panels), but a curmudgeon-on-curmudgeon would point out that we are explicitly hoisting under the cover of "deduplication" while net char count grows ~50% for the section. Defensible — and the EXP file anticipates and answers this objection — but reviewers should be aware.
holes_found.recommendation: No edit recommended; the EXP-436 author addressed this in anticipated_objections. Note it in the decider report so a future audit cycle sees the trade-off acknowledged.

stronger_arguments.description: The cluster paragraph could mention the SHARED Domain #11 explicitly. PROS-002 in raw-text/02-wins.txt:22 points to → WIN-007 + WIN-036, and WIN-006/WIN-022 inherit the same parent-prediction chain. Domain #11's code services this whole cluster. Saying so in the cluster paragraph would (a) ground the "hardcoded target" claim concretely ("Domain #11 in monitor.py"), (b) explain why WIN-022's "no monitor.py entry" framing means no DEDICATED entry — the cluster shares one domain, and WIN-022's phase-transition claim isn't tested anywhere inside it.

The paragraph could mention IGRF-14 release date (November 2024) symmetric to WMM2025's December 2024 — both are model products with public release dates that long predate any plausible dome monitor commit for these WINs. This would strengthen the "long before any dome monitor commit" assertion with a second concrete date stamp.
sources: NOAA / IAGA IGRF-14 release announcement Nov 2024

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-436 is the right move for the right reason. The c5/c6 cycles correctly identified that WIN-006/007/022/036 each had a ~600-1100 char post-hoc paragraph repeating the same NP.xy dataset audit, the same hardcoded-monitor framing, and the same legitimate-novelty carve-out quote — and a reader scrolling the WINs page would experience this as four independent post-hoc arguments when it is one cluster-level argument applied four times. Hoisting the shared argument to Part 3.1b () gives it room to be told once with full force and lets the per-WIN panels carry only WIN-specific evidence — the WIN-006 0.0466 deg/yr target, the WIN-007 Chulliat & Newitt 2010 EOS citation, the WIN-022 monitor.py-absence finding. The explicit separation of WIN-036 is also correct: its post-hoc failure routes through the OpenTimestamps canary ( — OTS bundles prediction-side and observation-side fields, hash does not prove temporal ordering) and the WMM2025 pre-publication arithmetic (139.298°E from Dec 2024, PROS-006 registered Mar 2026, +14 months), neither of which belongs in a hardcoded-monitor cluster paragraph. The EXP-436 author's own anticipated_objections section already addresses the strongest counterargument ("why hoist if net chars grew?") with the TF-IDF response.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Being right about the consolidation doesn't save the residual presentation tensions. The opening "same source: NP.xy" overstates by flattening WMM2025 into NP.xy (); the WIN-007 "acceleration target" wording () is more specific than what is actually hardcoded; and the WIN-022 "no monitor.py entry exists" line () preserves a pre-existing tension with that WIN's own detail_evidence ("Domain #11 in monitor.py uses a hardcoded exponential decay formula"). These are minor polish issues — the cluster argument itself is correct and the consolidation is the right structural move — but they are the kind of seams a defender will probe first, and they would have been easier to fix during the EXP than after.

deeper_analysis: Three structural observations beyond the listed holes. (1) Cross-tab linking pattern: the WIN-side back-references use bare <a href="#posthoc-npxy"> (no onclick=showTab), while the Part 3.1b paragraph's forward references to WIN-006/007/022/036 use the onclick="showTab('wins')" pattern. Both work — the global hashchange handler in showTab.js auto-switches tabs based on the target element's parent .ds-tab-content — but the asymmetry is worth a stylesheet-conventions ISS at some point. The existing #posthoc-standard back-references (54 hits across wins.json) all use the bare-href pattern, so EXP-436 is consistent with prior convention; just noting it. (2) WIN-036 inclusion in the cluster intro but exclusion from the "three per-WIN verdict panels reference this paragraph" line is correctly framed but slightly awkward — a first-time reader sees WIN-036 listed in the four-WIN set, then sees only three of the four reference back. The paragraph handles this by explicitly explaining ("WIN-036 is treated separately on its own verdict panel because its post-hoc failure runs through the OpenTimestamps canary"), which is the right move. (3) The Part 3.1b TLDR (in the <summary> wrapping the <h3>) is unchanged. The new worked-example paragraph lives inside the expandable ps-detail block and a reader who never expands it never sees the cluster argument at all. This is fine for the WINs-page reader (they get the compressed reference linking back), but a reader who starts at Part 3.1b in the review-tab TOC sees only the TLDR teaser and the abstract framework — they would have to expand the details to see the worked example. Not blocking, but the TLDR could mention "see worked example below: the NOAA NP.xy cluster."

citations_verified: Chulliat & Newitt 2010 EOS — consistent with existing WIN-007 citation (DOI 10.1029/2010EO490001 in WIN-007 detail_evidence, not re-resolved this cycle but unchanged from c3)
WMM2025 release December 2024 — consistent with WMM2025 actual release Dec 17, 2024 (US/UK joint, NCEI announcement)
gufm1 pre-1900 / IGRF modern era blend — consistent with NOAA NGDC NP.xy product documentation; gufm1 (Jackson, Jonkers & Walker 2000) covers 1590-1990, NOAA blends it with IGRF at ~1900
IGRF-14 current generation — IGRF-14 released November 2024, current as of May 2026 (next generation IGRF-15 expected ~2029)
PROS-006 registered 2026-03-06 — consistent with raw-text/03-predictions.txt:40 and prior cycles
WMM2025 vs PROS-006 delta = "more than fourteen months" — Dec 17 2024 to Mar 6 2026 = ~14.6 months, accurate
citations_unchecked: NCEI WMM URL https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/world-magnetic-model — not re-fetched this cycle; assumed live

cross_win_consistency: Per-WIN tag consistency: WIN-006.c6 monitoring=live_fetch, post_hoc=true; WIN-007.c3 monitoring=live_fetch, post_hoc=true; WIN-022.c2 monitoring=none, post_hoc=true; WIN-036.c5 monitoring=hardcoded, post_hoc=true. The cluster paragraph correctly aggregates these (WIN-006/007 hardcoded-target on live-fetch commit; WIN-022 no entry; WIN-036 OTS-canary not hardcoded-monitor). The two minor inaccuracies flagged (NP.xy-vs-WMM2025 source flattening; WIN-007 acceleration-target wording) are surface-precision issues, not tag inconsistencies.
advocate_mode: Strongest dome-defender objection to EXP-436: "You consolidated four independent post-hoc arguments into one. That is not deduplication — that is admission that you were padding your case by repeating yourself. The cluster paragraph itself acknowledges 'rather than re-litigating the dataset audit each time' — which concedes that the original four-WIN treatment was litigating-the-same-point-four-times." Best response: the consolidation IS an admission that the four-WIN replication was a presentation weakness; the structural move now makes the cluster-level argument visible and easier to attack head-on (which is good for adversarial integrity). Saying-the-same-thing-four-times-louder is not the same as making-the-same-point-stronger; the EXP file's anticipated_objections section already addressed this and the response is honest. Second-best dome objection: "Including WIN-036 in the four-WIN intro but excluding it from the three-WIN reference structure is a category-error in your own taxonomy." Response: the paragraph explicitly handles this by routing WIN-036 to OTS canary; consistent and well-flagged. Third dome objection (the strongest unmitigated one): "You collapse NP.xy and WMM2025 into 'the same source' when they are distinct NOAA products." Response: this one bites; see hole #1 — could be tightened.
quantitative_verification: Length verification: EXP-436 claimed +2121 chars for Part 3.1b — measured Part 3.1b region 8726 chars vs prior ~6605 (consistent with +2121). EXP claimed WIN-006 compressed to ~507 chars and WIN-007 to ~504 and WIN-022 to ~492 in the new reference sentence; measured WIN-006 reference sentence ~510 chars, WIN-007 ~485 chars, WIN-022 ~510 chars (approximately consistent, within ~5%). Net delta +540 across four fields matches EXP estimate. Date arithmetic: WMM2025 release Dec 17, 2024 → PROS-006 registration Mar 6, 2026 = 14 months 18 days, correctly stated as "more than fourteen months." Threshold arithmetic: 139.298°E - 120°E = 19.298°, exceeds 18° threshold by 1.298° — correctly stated as "pure arithmetic." All quantitative claims verified.
summary_for_decider: EXP-436 (NPxy cluster hoist) is a successful and well-motivated section-level integration. Cluster argument is correct, WIN-036 explicit separation is well-justified, all quantitative claims verified, all anchors and cross-tab links work cleanly, tests pass (3693 passed, 0 failed). Four minor polish holes, none blocking: (1) opening "same source: NP.xy" should be softened to acknowledge WMM2025 is distinct; (2) WIN-007 compressed reference "acceleration target" is more specific than what is actually hardcoded — would be tighter as "longitude threshold + exponential-decay formula parameters" or simply "target number"; (3) WIN-022 compressed reference "no monitor.py entry exists" preserves pre-existing tension with WIN-022 detail_evidence describing Domain #11's exponential formula — should be reconciled in a separate ISS; (4) net char delta is +540 (Part 3.1b grew; per-WIN shrunk) — defensible per the TF-IDF argument and EXP's own anticipated_objections, but worth noting. Closes ISS-2214 (the c6 hole #2 that motivated the EXP). Recommended action: minor_edit (optional polish; not blocking). Two stronger-argument suggestions for future EXPs: mention Domain #11 explicitly in the cluster paragraph; mention IGRF-14 release date (Nov 2024) symmetric to WMM2025. queue_id 410 set; reviewing qid=410 (deep-attack, singleton) only; qid=411 (verification, part2b) deferred to next run per Step 0b default rule.
